level_signals: 0→1 and scaling role with ownership of core voice surface
Expected to engage deeply with engineers on system design

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of product management experience, with meaningful exposure to real-time systems, voice, or AI products
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful exposure to real-time systems, voice, or AI products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience shipping voice or real-time products - You understand the constraints of latency, streaming systems, and user expectations in synchronous interactions
Strong technical depth - Ability to engage deeply with engineers on system design (e.g., speech pipelines, streaming infra, telephony systems, reliability tradeoffs)
Experience working with AI systems - Familiarity with LLMs, speech-to-text, or text-to-speech systems and their limitations in production environments
Track record of 0→1 product development - Comfortable operating in ambiguous spaces and iterating quickly to reach product-market fit

scope.surface: Voice product — real-time AI conversations across telephony and streaming systems
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS platform for AI-powered customer experiences
core_responsibilities: Define the voice interaction model - Shape how agents handle real-time conversations—turn-taking, interruptions, latency, tone, and recovery from errors.
Build reliable real-time systems - Work closely with engineering on streaming architectures, latency budgets, and failure handling.
Own the voice stack experience - Partner across ASR, TTS, LLMs, and telephony integrations to deliver a cohesive product.
Make voice measurable and improvable - Define how we evaluate voice agents: latency, interruption handling, resolution rate, and conversation quality.
Translate real-world usage into product direction - Work closely with customers deploying voice agents in production.

compensation.equity_form: Eligible full-time employees can participate in Sierra's equity plans subject to the terms of the applicable plans and policies.

green_flags: Clear product surface with specific ownership (Voice end-to-end)
Well-known co-founders (Bret Taylor, Clay Bavor) with strong AI/tech pedigree
Zero-to-one and scaling role — high impact potential
Explicit encouragement to apply even if experience doesn't precisely match
Company values section is detailed and genuine-sounding, not generic
Equity participation offered to full-time employees
red_flags: Primarily in-person company based in San Francisco — limits remote candidates
No explicit seniority level stated; 3+ years could be interpreted as mid-level but scope suggests senior expectations

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + scope of role; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; partially stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'primarily an in-person company based in San Francisco')
ambiguities: The JD lists offices in Atlanta, New York, London, Paris, Madrid, Munich, Singapore, Japan, and Sydney but the role's hiring location is not explicitly scoped to San Francisco only — could be open to other offices. Treated as San Francisco primary given 'primarily in-person company based in San Francisco'.
No mention of visa sponsorship anywhere in the JD; treated as 'unclear'.
Nice-to-haves section is absent; all listed qualifications in 'What you'll bring' appear unconditional and treated as must-haves.
